content.problemStatement:
In this example, we are going to demonstrate a simple simulation of a system that continuously checks for new updates using an infinite loop in C. The system indicates that it is operational by repeatedly displaying the message **"Checking for new updates".**

**You will get a Time limit exceeded error because we have not terminated the loop.
To get AC (Accepted) in this problem, use break inside the while loop — it immediately stops the loop. We’ll discuss more about break later on.**

**When executed, the code will show:**
```default
Checking for new updates...
```
